ranked_gaps.relation_to_cage: Direct synergy and the single most natural extension of CAGE. CAGE already is training-free agent-judge adversarial co-evolution with a certified endpoint and an explicit deception-premium; this gap simply asks whether that certificate holds across the whole evolutionary trajectory rather than at one endpoint. It reuses CAGE's core machinery (adversarial judge, certification, deception measurement) and lifts it to the longitudinal regime - a deepening, not a pivot.
ranked_gaps.crowdedness: Low-to-emerging. Generic 'meta-evaluation' and 'calibration-as-reward' papers began appearing in 2026 (arXiv 2601.21464, 2606.14211 reflection-calibration, 2607.00871 anytime-valid certificates), so the label is no longer empty - but the adversarial, deception-premium, certified-endpoint framing that CAGE owns is still whitespace and gives clear differentiation from the calibration-bonus crowd.

ranked_gaps.gap: No contamination firewall or process-level measurement for agents that write their own memory/skills/prompts. The contaminated unit is the agent's evolving STATE (not a static dataset), so you cannot dedup against a corpus and any held-out is only unseen until the first stream touches it. There is no operational definition of 'improving' vs plateau vs collapse and no standard separating genuine accumulation from laundered test exposure ('memory in a costume').
ranked_gaps.why_biggest: Recurs across all four lenses (surveys gap 3; methods gap 8; evaluation gaps 1,2,4,7; deployment gap 4) and is explicitly named the 'single most foundational gap - without it no self-improvement number is falsifiable.' It gates progress on every other gap because you cannot optimize or certify what you cannot measure. The ~37% lab-to-production drop and the 'wipe test' failures show current numbers are systematically inflated.
ranked_gaps.contribution_if_solved: A reusable, adopted measurement substrate: a formal notion of 'information the agent could not have absorbed' plus freshly-generated per-checkpoint held-out tasks, a standardized wipe-test-plus-regeneration protocol, and process-level metrics (retention, forward/backward transfer, per-unit-of-experience gain, collapse onset). This would become the field's evaluation standard the way ImageNet/held-out hygiene did for earlier eras.
ranked_gaps.tractable_by_small_group: Yes - the most tractable high-leverage item. It is methodology/benchmark/infrastructure work: cheap relative to model training, does not require frontier compute, and a single group that ships a clean firewall protocol plus harness can define the standard. The hard part is intellectual (formalizing absorbable information), not resource-bound.
ranked_gaps.concrete_first_paper: 'A Contamination Firewall for Self-Modifying Agents.' Formalize 'absorbable information' relative to an agent's write/retrieval history; build the first per-checkpoint freshly-generated held-out generator provably outside that history; pair it with a standardized wipe-test-plus-regeneration and a null/random-improvement baseline; demonstrate on 2-3 existing self-evolving methods that a large fraction of reported gains are test-laundering that vanishes under the firewall. Release as an open harness.
ranked_gaps.relation_to_cage: Synergy. CAGE needs exactly this to make its 'certified endpoint' claim falsifiable and contamination-proof; the firewall is the evaluation half of the certification story. It is a complementary skill-set (protocol/stats) rather than a new adversarial mechanism, so it broadens CAGE without pivoting away from it.
ranked_gaps.crowdedness: Medium on the surface (a 2025-2026 benchmark wave: SEA-Eval, SEAGym, LifelongAgentBench, Evo-Memory, StuLife) but LOW on the actual firewall: every one of those admits its held-out is drawn from the same static corpus the agent can pattern-match, and none operationalizes an unreachable held-out. The whitespace is the firewall itself, not yet-another benchmark.

ranked_gaps.gap: No evolution-invariant safety kernel: no theory or mechanism guaranteeing that an agent rewriting its own skills/memory/prompts preserves a fixed set of safety/value invariants across an unbounded number of self-edits, when the safety monitor is itself inside the optimization scope and therefore evolvable/route-around-able.
ranked_gaps.why_biggest: Recurs across three lenses (surveys gap 5; methods gap 7 - flagged 'deepest, least-crowded'; deployment gap 1 - 'deepest, least-crowded bet') and is the one whose failure is catastrophic and irreversible (Lamarckian propagation, capability ratchet, DGM's demonstrated removal of its own hallucination markers). It is the two-horned bind of the field: fixed extrinsic meta-loops provably plateau, intrinsic self-modification is empirically unsafe and unverifiable.
ranked_gaps.contribution_if_solved: A tamper-resistant invariant anchor plus a proof/certification technique for 'safety preserved under this class of self-updates' - the missing precondition for any legally deployable self-modifying agent under EU AI Act 'reassess on every material change.' Even a partial result (invariant persistence for a restricted, well-characterized class of edits) would be foundational.
ranked_gaps.tractable_by_small_group: Partially - be honest here. A restricted, formally-characterized edit class with an architectural immutable anchor, demonstrated on a DGM-style self-rewriting agent, is achievable by a strong group. But full guarantees where the verifier itself evolves, combinatorial emergent behavior, and population-level (Simpson's-paradox) safety likely need scale and multi-year effort; a small group should deliberately scope to the tractable restricted-class version and resist over-claiming.
ranked_gaps.concrete_first_paper: 'An Immutable Invariant That Self-Modification Cannot Route Around.' Define a minimal class of self-edits and a small set of invariants; build a tamper-evident architectural anchor outside the agent's editable substrate; replicate the DGM marker-removal exploit and show baseline monitors are gamed within N generations while the kernel's invariant provably (or empirically) survives. Ship the exploit reproduction plus the kernel as the contribution.
ranked_gaps.relation_to_cage: Half-extension, half-pivot. CAGE's 'certified endpoint' is conceptually a static invariant; extending it to a persistent-across-generations safety invariant reuses the certification mindset but pulls toward formal methods / systems architecture and away from the adversarial-judge core. A defensible adjacent second front, not the core bet.
ranked_gaps.crowdedness: Low but rising fast. 2026 brought architectural containment proposals (IBAAM, SafeHarness 2604.13630, Agent libOS 2606.03895, SafeAgent) - so the systems/containment angle is filling in, but true immutability guarantees and persistence proofs remain open. Move soon or the architecture side crowds.

ranked_gaps.gap: No theory of non-degrading (monotone or stably-converging) experiential self-improvement, and no ONLINE detector that fires before capability is already lost. Self-training on self-generated experience empirically shows rise-and-collapse (e.g. 23.2%->8.5% over three rounds), entropy/diversity collapse, and model autophagy, with anti-collapse results proven only in toy linear regimes.
ranked_gaps.why_biggest: Recurs across three-plus lenses (surveys gap 2; methods gaps 2&4; evaluation's self-regress landmark; deployment's statistical-limits/diversity-collapse). It is the dynamical-systems core of the field - the question of when experiential loops converge vs saturate vs diverge - and it is entangled with gap 1 (bad signal drives the collapse).
ranked_gaps.contribution_if_solved: A characterization of the phase boundary as a function of measurable factors (signal verifiability, diversity/entropy, update frequency, regression tolerance) plus an online early-warning statistic that detects collapse onset before capability loss - directly actionable as a safety/stopping mechanism in any deployed loop.
ranked_gaps.tractable_by_small_group: Yes. Collapse can be reproduced in controlled loops at modest scale, and an online detector is a concrete, testable artifact. The theory can start empirical/phenomenological (measure the boundary) before demanding a full proof, which keeps it in reach.
ranked_gaps.concrete_first_paper: 'Predicting Rise-and-Collapse Before It Happens.' Reproduce documented collapse cases across a few domains; map the improve/saturate/collapse phase boundary against measurable predictors; build and validate an out-of-loop early-warning statistic (e.g. learnable-information-gain / entropy-decay signature) that raises an alarm strictly before the accuracy cliff. Deliver the detector plus the phase diagram.
ranked_gaps.relation_to_cage: Adjacent pivot. It shares CAGE's out-of-loop-certification philosophy (an external signal watching the evolving system) but studies dynamics/stability rather than adversarial judging. Complements gap 1 well - a collapse detector and a signal-decoupling detector are two instruments on the same dashboard - but is a distinct research thrust.
ranked_gaps.crowdedness: Medium and heating up. 2026 produced multiple collapse-characterization papers (2606.21090 rise-and-collapse, misevolution 2509.26354) and model-collapse theory is established. Whitespace is the PREDICTIVE online detector and the real-agentic-loop phase theory, not another collapse-demonstration.

ranked_gaps.gap: Long-horizon, multi-session credit assignment and provenance for experiential improvement: given thousands of memory writes / skill edits over a lifetime, no reliable way to attribute an outcome to the specific experience that caused it, nor an attribution substrate recording source, validity, conflict, and downstream influence.
ranked_gaps.why_biggest: Recurs across three lenses (surveys gap 4; methods gap 3; deployment gap 3-provenance/auditability). It is the mechanism that would let you localize and surgically forget the item causing a regression - a prerequisite for both stable improvement and legally-required auditability - and it is uniquely hard because memory writes make trajectories non-comparable (Memory-R2's insight).
ranked_gaps.contribution_if_solved: An attribution substrate + counterfactual credit estimator that localizes which stored experience drove a regression or a gain, enabling targeted forgetting, tamper-evident provenance linking each behavior to the self-update that caused it, and honest per-decision accounting over long horizons.
ranked_gaps.tractable_by_small_group: Mostly yes for the substrate + session-wise counterfactual estimator (local re-rollouts from matched memory states); the fully general lifelong RL credit-assignment problem is not, so scope to the memory-attribution version. Achievable with existing agent frameworks and modest compute.
ranked_gaps.concrete_first_paper: 'Attribution Substrate for Self-Evolving Memory.' Instrument every memory/skill write with provenance (write context, retrieval usage, downstream outcome); build a session-wise counterfactual credit estimator that re-rolls from matched memory states to fairly compare; show it localizes the specific stored experience responsible for a demonstrated regression and that targeted forgetting of that item restores performance.
ranked_gaps.relation_to_cage: Pivot. Credit assignment is orthogonal to CAGE's adversarial-judge core - it shares the 'certify what happened' spirit but the machinery (provenance, counterfactual re-rollouts) is new territory. Best treated as a possible later extension, not the multi-year thesis.
ranked_gaps.crowdedness: Mixed. RL long-horizon credit assignment is crowded (47+ papers, a poor differentiator). The memory-provenance / attribution-substrate angle (2606.04990) is less crowded and more distinctive - specialize there if entering, but it is not the highest-whitespace-per-effort bet.

ranked_gaps.gap: Human oversight treated as fixed and exogenous when it is endogenous and non-stationary: sustained interaction erodes the human's independent evaluative capacity, human preferences drift while the agent freezes them, and agent-iteration vs human-adaptation run on mismatched timescales - so the agent optimizes against a moving, degrading supervisor with no mechanism to maintain it.
ranked_gaps.why_biggest: Genuinely foundational and the least-crowded framing in the corpus, but recurrence is only two lenses (surveys gap 6 via the Microsoft position paper; deployment). Its foundational size is high (it reframes the entire evaluation-signal question as a two-coupled-adaptive-systems problem) which is why it ranks despite thin recurrence.
ranked_gaps.contribution_if_solved: A joint longitudinal model + measurement of whether agent evolution degrades its human evaluator, and mechanisms to maintain oversight bandwidth over weeks-to-years - the missing evaluation layer for exactly the value-laden domains where verifiers fail and high-bandwidth human steering is most needed.
ranked_gaps.tractable_by_small_group: Weakly - be honest. It requires longitudinal human-subjects studies (the agent changes between sessions vs frozen; measure oversight-calibration drift over weeks), which is slow, IRB-bound, and hard to make reproducible. A small group can do a first controlled study but cannot easily reach the multi-year, real-deployment horizon where the effect is strongest. This tractability penalty is why it is ranked sixth despite superb whitespace.
ranked_gaps.concrete_first_paper: 'Does Improving the Agent Degrade the Overseer?' A controlled study where users supervise an agent that either changes between sessions (evolving) or is frozen; measure the human's independent judgment calibration over sessions; test whether agent evolution silently erodes human oversight and whether a scaffold (forced periodic blind re-verification) restores it.
ranked_gaps.relation_to_cage: Pivot. The human-as-endogenous-verifier idea rhymes with CAGE's judge-centric worldview (the human is just a non-stationary judge), but the methods (human-subjects, HCI, longitudinal design) are far from CAGE's training-free adversarial-agent core. Interesting long-term horizon, wrong near-term fit.
ranked_gaps.crowdedness: Very low - the strongest whitespace of any gap here (only the Microsoft position paper and a couple of 2026 entrants like 2606.06114 'Towards Healthy Evolution'). The bottleneck is tractability, not competition.

adjudication.the_one_recommendation: Specialize in LONGITUDINAL CERTIFICATION OF THE IMPROVEMENT SIGNAL for self-evolving agents (gap 1): a multi-year program building the theory, instruments, and certified stopping rules that answer 'does the signal driving self-improvement stay trustworthy - uncaptured by the agent's own optimization - as the agent evolves?' This is the honest single answer for three reasons. (1) Recurrence: it is the only gap that independently surfaces in all four lenses, and it is causally upstream of almost every other gap - reward hacking, model collapse, safety drift, and 'memory in a costume' are all downstream symptoms of an uncertified signal. (2) CAGE fit: it is the natural deepening of your existing work, not a pivot. CAGE already does training-free agent-judge adversarial co-evolution with a certified endpoint and a deception-premium; this program simply lifts that certificate from a single endpoint to the full evolutionary trajectory and asks when it provably holds. You keep your adversarial-judge machinery and your certification lens; you add anytime-valid sequential statistics and an out-of-loop meta-verifier. (3) Tractability x whitespace: it is buildable by one well-resourced academic group on verifiable-domain testbeds plus API models (no frontier training), and while generic 'meta-evaluation' and 'calibration bonus' papers started appearing in 2026, the adversarial deception-premium + certified-endpoint framing is still open and is precisely your differentiator. Anchor the program with the gap-2 contamination firewall as its evaluation backbone (so your certification claims are themselves falsifiable), and treat gap-3 (persistent safety invariants) and gap-4 (online collapse detection) as adjacent second fronts that reuse the same out-of-loop-certifier philosophy. Net: one coherent identity - 'certified, collapse-resistant improvement signals for self-evolving agents' - that grows straight out of CAGE and owns a foundational, still-uncrowded question.
adjudication.what_to_avoid: 1) Yet another agent MEMORY ARCHITECTURE or SKILL-LIBRARY retrieval/curation scheme (A-Mem, Mem0, MemGen, Memento, SKILL.md variants). Explicitly flagged as saturated across all four analyses; the wipe-test and harness-updating-is-not-harness-benefit results show storage/retrieval is not the binding constraint. Hundreds of papers compete for shrinking deltas. 2) Generic LLM-AS-JUDGE RELIABILITY / self-preference-bias studies WITHOUT the longitudinal, adversarial, under-optimization framing - a large static-setting literature already exists (IRT diagnosis, self-bias surveys); the value is only in what happens to the judge across iterations while the agent games it. 3) Another STATIC or single-world BENCHMARK that scores endpoint success - the field already has a 2025-2026 benchmark glut; do not add to it unless you are shipping the contamination firewall / process-level measurement it all lacks. 4) Incremental RLVR WEIGHT-UPDATE methods and classical CATASTROPHIC-FORGETTING/continual-learning adapters (replay, EWC, LoRA) ported to agents - crowded, and Yue et al. suggest RLVR mostly re-weights within the base model's frontier, capping the ceiling. 5) MULTI-AGENT ORCHESTRATION / population co-evolution hype as a primary bet - diversity-collapse and contagion results show it is unstable and the failures are emergent/collective, making clean small-group progress hard. 6) Single-shot SELF-REFLECTION PROMPT tricks (Reflexion descendants) - capped by the base model's self-evaluation ability and known to plateau after one pass. 7) Grand RECURSIVE-SELF-IMPROVEMENT / intrinsic-metacognition manifestos without a measurable, verifiable-domain testbed - intellectually seductive but currently unfalsifiable and unsafe to demonstrate; only enter via the certified-signal or immutable-invariant sub-problems that are actually instrumentable.
